summary: Eliminated the streaming-to-settled transition flash by making the streaming and settled states share a single stable DOM node. Previously, LiveStreamingBlock was a separate element appended after the <For> list — when streaming ended, a new MessageBubble would appear while LiveStreamingBlock faded out, causing a visible position jump. The fix: (1) In useAgent.run(), pre-generate the assistant message ID and add a placeholder message to the session store BEFORE calling rustAgent.run(), so the <For> list has a DOM node for the live response from the very first token. (2) When streaming ends, call session.updateMessage() with the final content instead of session.addMessage() — same ID means same DOM node, no unmount/remount. (3) Removed LiveStreamingBlock from MessageList entirely and wired agent.streamingContent, agent.activeToolCalls(), and agent.thinkingSegments() directly into the last MessageRow via the new streamingContent/streamingToolCalls/streamingThinkingSegments props. (4) Updated MessageBubble to render thinking segments and a typing-dot indicator during streaming mode. (5) Extended the DB updateMessage function to persist toolCalls, costUSD, and error fields, and wired it into the store's updateMessage so settled messages are fully persisted. Edge cases: 0 tokens (agent produces no text) — placeholder is deleted cleanly. Cancel — placeholder is updated with partial content or deleted. Errors — placeholder is updated with error field. All transitions are atomic in SolidJS's reactive scheduler.
